texto: What had he done? Something horrible, something terrible, but something he'd done as a child. Can you commit murder in innocence? It's too big a thing for the human mind to take in, that's the problem. And it grew with the ever-larger newspaper pictures of a girl who was near enough an angel, even before she died. Only the young die good. And Angela Milton died young enough to be perfect.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jonathan Trigell que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “What had he done? Something horrible, something terrible, but something he'd done as a child. Can…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, angel, dying-young, com foco em dying-young e innocence.
